tasks.title: Provision Postgres 16 + pgvector locally
acceptance_criteria: Either apt-installed postgres-16 + postgresql-16-pgvector on the dev host, OR a docker-compose.yml under deploy/postgres/ that brings the same stack up
pg_isready returns 0 against the configured PG_DSN
SELECT extversion FROM pg_extension WHERE extname='vector' returns >= '0.5.0' (HNSW support)
Database 'ema_nlp' exists; superuser role for ingest documented in deploy/postgres/README.md
PG_DSN entry added to ~/.myenvs/ema_nlp.env example (without secrets)

tasks.title: Schema DDL + idempotent init script
acceptance_criteria: corpus/pg_schema.sql contains the three-table schema from exploration.md §Schema (documents, chunks, links) including documents.committee column and HNSW + GIN indexes
scripts/init_db.py runs the DDL idempotently (CREATE … IF NOT EXISTS); --reset flag drops and re-creates tables
After running init_db, \dt shows documents/chunks/links; \d chunks confirms the HNSW index and the tsvector generated column
config.py exposes PG_DSN from env with a safe default for local dev

tasks.title: Python deps + connection pool + pgvector type registration
acceptance_criteria: pyproject.toml adds: psycopg[binary], psycopg_pool, pgvector, trafilatura
harness/pg/conn.py exposes get_pool() with lazy psycopg_pool init and registers the pgvector type on every new connection
harness/pg/queries.py skeleton contains parameterised SQL string constants used by later tasks (no logic yet)
Smoke test passes: a Python REPL can connect, run SELECT 1, and round-trip a 1024-dim vector

tasks.title: Configurable LlamaIndex-backed chunker
acceptance_criteria: corpus/ingestion/chunker.py exposes ChunkConfig(parser, max_tokens, overlap, min_chunk_chars) and chunk_markdown(text, config) -> list[Chunk]
parser='markdown' uses LlamaIndex MarkdownNodeParser with SentenceSplitter fallback for nodes over max_tokens
parser='sentence' uses SentenceSplitter directly; parser='hierarchical' uses HierarchicalNodeParser
Chunks below min_chunk_chars are dropped
Each returned Chunk carries text, heading_path, token_count, chunk_index
Unit test on a fixture EMA PDF markdown emits ≥2 chunks with heading_path populated

tasks.title: PDF document normaliser + metadata extraction
acceptance_criteria: corpus/ingestion/pdf_normaliser.py exposes normalise_pdf_doc(mongo_doc) -> DocumentInput

DocumentInput captures: source_url, title (H1 or filename fallback), reference_number (regex EMA/.../YYYY), committee (parsed from reference_number; CHMP/PRAC/CVMP/COMP/PDCO/CAT), revision, last_updated, topic_path (URL path segment)
Returns None gracefully when markdown is empty or error field non-empty
Unit test on a fixture mongo doc returns expected DocumentInput; missing-fields case yields safe defaults

tasks.title: BGE embedder wrapper (LlamaIndex on CUDA)
acceptance_criteria: harness/embed_pg.py::Embedder uses harness/providers.py to configure BGE-large-en-v1.5 via LlamaIndex Settings
Autodetects CUDA; logs the device used at startup
Batch encode method takes list[str] and returns list[list[float]] of 1024-dim
Smoke test: encodes a batch of 8 strings; tensor shape and dtype as expected

tasks.title: Ingest pipeline: PDFs → chunks → embeddings → upsert
acceptance_criteria: harness/embed_pg.py exposes ingest_source(source: Literal['pdfs','html'], batch_size, limit, force)
CLI: python -m harness.embed_pg --source pdfs [--batch-size N] [--limit N] [--force]
Streams parsed_pdfs from MongoDB (filter {error: ''}), normalises, chunks, embeds in batches, bulk-upserts into documents + chunks
chunk_id is sha256(doc_id || chunk_index || normalised_text); INSERT ON CONFLICT (chunk_id) DO NOTHING
tqdm progress bar; per-document try/except so one bad doc does not kill the run
Limit-10 dry run completes successfully and produces at least 10 rows in documents and ≥10 rows in chunks

tasks.title: Resume + dedup verification + --force semantics
acceptance_criteria: Re-running the same --limit N command produces zero new chunks (ON CONFLICT verified)
--force deletes all chunks for the affected source URLs before re-inserting
After --force, chunks counts match a fresh run on the same slice
Integration test scripts/test_ingest_resume.py covers both paths

tasks.title: HTML normaliser via trafilatura
acceptance_criteria: corpus/ingestion/html_normaliser.py exposes normalise_html(html, url) -> DocumentInput | None
Uses trafilatura.extract with output_format='markdown', include_links=True, with_metadata=True
Returns None for landing/nav pages where extracted text < 200 chars
Title and date come from trafilatura metadata when available; fallback to URL path segment
Unit test: a content fixture HTML returns markdown ≥200 chars; a nav-only fixture returns None

tasks.title: Extend ingest pipeline to HTML source
acceptance_criteria: ingest_source(source='html') reads from web_items where content_type='text/html'
Pipes html_raw → html_normaliser → chunker → embedder → upsert (same path as PDFs)
Skipped landing pages are logged with URL and reason
Limit-10 dry run produces documents with source_type='html' in Postgres

tasks.title: Timing + scaling notes
acceptance_criteria: Run --source pdfs --limit 100 and --source html --limit 100 on the 3090 machine
.claude/work/2026-05-25_16_pgvector-narrative-corpus/timing_notes.md captures: GPU memory, throughput (docs/min), extrapolated full-run wall time, disk usage of pgvector index
Doc names the bottleneck (embedding vs. SQL upsert vs. chunking)

tasks.title: Link extractor module
acceptance_criteria: corpus/ingestion/link_extractor.py exposes Link dataclass (tgt_url, link_type, anchor) and three extractors:
- extract_from_markdown(md) returns Links with link_type='hyperlink' from [text](url) patterns
- extract_from_html(html, base_url) walks <a href>, resolves relative URLs against base_url
- extract_reference_numbers(text) regex-extracts EMA/.../YYYY codes as link_type='reference_number'
- extract_see_qa(text) regex-extracts 'see Q&A N' / 'see question N' patterns as link_type='see_qa'
Unit tests cover each extractor with positive + negative cases

tasks.title: Wire link extraction into ingest
acceptance_criteria: ingest_source runs all extractors on each document and bulk-inserts into links
Each link row carries src_doc_id and the chunk_id of the chunk that contained the link (best-effort)
INSERT … ON CONFLICT (src_doc_id, tgt_url, link_type) DO NOTHING; rerun is a no-op
After ingesting a small slice, SELECT link_type, COUNT(*) FROM links GROUP BY 1 shows non-zero rows for hyperlink and reference_number

tasks.title: Reference-number → URL resolution pass
acceptance_criteria: scripts/resolve_links.py fills links.tgt_doc_id where it is NULL
Resolution rules: tgt_url that matches documents.source_url; tgt_url (reference code) that matches documents.reference_number
Idempotent — re-running updates only rows still NULL
Reports counts: resolved hyperlinks, resolved references, remaining unresolved (with sample)
Documented expectation: hyperlink resolution >= 30% on a full ingest (many links point off-site)

tasks.title: RetrievalConfigPG + LlamaIndex adapter scaffolding
acceptance_criteria: harness/retrieve_pg.py defines RetrievalConfigPG, PrefilterConfig (topic_path_prefix, committee, date_range), TraversalConfig (mode, max_hops, link_types)
RetrievalConfigPG.from_yaml_section reads the retrieval: block from a config YAML and round-trips through a unit test
harness/pg/adapter.py converts a list[RetrievalResult] from pg into LlamaIndex NodeWithScore objects so existing workflows accept them
Adapter exposes get_node_by_id(chunk_id) that hits chunks table on demand — replaces VectorStoreIndex.docstore.get_node

tasks.title: Dense retrieval over pgvector
acceptance_criteria: retrieve_dense_pg(query, config) embeds the query, runs an HNSW kNN search via the <=> operator, joins documents for metadata
Pre-filter clauses (topic_path_prefix LIKE, committee = ANY, last_updated BETWEEN) are applied in the WHERE before LIMIT
Returns ordered list[RetrievalResult] = (chunk_id, score, metadata) where score is 1 - cosine_distance
p50 latency ≤ 200 ms for k=10 on the full corpus (measured in NARR-011 follow-up)
Unit test against a seeded test DB returns expected top-k for a known fixture

tasks.title: BM25 retrieval via Postgres tsvector
acceptance_criteria: retrieve_bm25_pg(query, config) uses ts_rank_cd(text_tsv, plainto_tsquery('english', $query)) and the same prefilter machinery as NARR-016
Returns ordered list[RetrievalResult]
Unit test against the same seeded test DB returns sensible results for a keyword-heavy query

tasks.title: Hybrid (RRF) + dispatcher + build_retrieve_fn_pg
acceptance_criteria: retrieve_hybrid_pg runs dense + bm25 retrievers concurrently and RRF-fuses with K=60 (same constant as harness/retrieve.py)
retrieve_with_config_pg(config, query) is the single entry point that dispatches on config.mode
build_retrieve_fn_pg(config) returns a callable matching the signature of harness.retrieve.build_retrieve_fn (drop-in)
Unit test confirms hybrid output length ≤ k and contains a union of dense + bm25 hits

tasks.title: Auto-traversal (recursive CTE over links)
acceptance_criteria: When TraversalConfig.mode='auto', initial top-k seeds a recursive CTE that walks links up to max_hops, filtered by link_types whitelist
Walk returns chunks for neighbour docs (one chunk per neighbour, highest-scoring or first); deduplicated by chunk_id
Initial top-k preserved at the front of the returned list; expanded neighbours appended
Unit test on a fixture link graph confirms 1-hop expansion behaves as designed; max_hops=0 is a no-op

tasks.title: follow_links agent tool for ReAct workflows
acceptance_criteria: harness/pg/tools.py exposes follow_links(chunk_id: str, link_types: list[str] | None, k: int) -> list[RetrievalResult]
Registered as a LlamaIndex FunctionTool consumed by harness/workflows/react_native.py when TraversalConfig.mode='agent_tool'
Tool docstring describes the contract clearly (input shape, return shape, when to use)
Smoke test: ReAct workflow runs end-to-end on one question, optionally invokes follow_links, no crash

tasks.title: Env-var dispatch in app.py
acceptance_criteria: EMA_RETRIEVER env var (default 'faiss') switches between harness.retrieve.build_retrieve_fn and harness.retrieve_pg.build_retrieve_fn_pg
When 'pgvector': skip build_index (no FAISS load), still satisfy the workflow signature via the adapter
EMA_RETRIEVER=faiss chainlit run app.py works exactly as before (back-compat check)
EMA_RETRIEVER=pgvector chainlit run app.py loads without index files and answers a sample question end-to-end

tasks.title: Env-var dispatch in run_eval.py + Phoenix backend attribute
acceptance_criteria: harness/run_eval.py honours EMA_RETRIEVER the same way as app.py
All workflows include a new Phoenix span attribute ema.retrieval.backend = 'faiss' | 'pgvector'
Run a small eval slice (≤5 questions) under both backends; both produce valid results/<run_id>/ outputs

tasks.title: End-to-end smoke test: simple_rag on pgvector
acceptance_criteria: Run simple_rag against 5 benchmark questions using EMA_RETRIEVER=pgvector
5/5 produce a non-empty answer
Phoenix project shows traces with ema.retrieval.backend='pgvector'
Spot check: at least one answer cites a chunk whose source_url is NOT in corpus.jsonl (confirms narrative coverage)

tasks.title: Expose prefilter + traversal in YAML configs
acceptance_criteria: RetrievalConfigPG.from_yaml_section reads retrieval.prefilter.{topic_path_prefix,committee,date_range} and retrieval.traversal.{mode,max_hops,link_types}
harness/configs/example_chmp_only.yaml ships as a reference
Eval run with committee=['CHMP'] returns only chunks whose documents.committee='CHMP' (verified by SQL count post-run)

tasks.title: Unit test suite — chunker, normalisers, link extractor
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_chunker.py, tests/test_pdf_normaliser.py, tests/test_html_normaliser.py, tests/test_link_extractor.py all green
Fixtures live under tests/fixtures/ema_pdf_sample.md, ema_html_sample.html, ema_nav_landing.html
Coverage on the four new modules ≥ 80% (measured via pytest-cov)
pytest -q runs in < 30 s for these tests

tasks.title: Integration test — retrieve_pg end-to-end
acceptance_criteria: tests/test_retrieve_pg.py uses a dedicated test database (env: PG_DSN_TEST) populated by a tiny fixture corpus of ~10 chunks across 3 documents with 2 links
Tests cover: dense top-k correctness, bm25 keyword hit, hybrid RRF combination, prefilter (committee), traversal mode='auto'
Test setup/teardown leaves no residue; tests are reentrant
CI documentation in docs/RETRIEVAL_PG.md notes how to provision the test DB

tasks.title: Documentation — CLAUDE.md + RETRIEVAL_PG.md
acceptance_criteria: CLAUDE.md §Data sources and §V1 scope locks updated: retrieval is over Postgres chunks; corpus.jsonl is benchmark-only; full narrative corpus is in scope
New docs/RETRIEVAL_PG.md covers: Postgres setup, env vars (PG_DSN, EMA_RETRIEVER), schema overview, ingest CLI, retrieval modes, traversal modes, sub-corpus filters, troubleshooting
docs/SETUP.md cross-links docs/RETRIEVAL_PG.md
.claude/HISTORY.md gets one row summarising the pivot

tasks.title: Switch default EMA_RETRIEVER to pgvector
acceptance_criteria: Default for EMA_RETRIEVER becomes 'pgvector' in config.py / app.py / run_eval.py
FAISS path remains available as 'faiss' and is marked legacy in its docstrings
All tests pass on the new default
.claude/HISTORY.md updated with the cutover date
